answer:
Multiple open-source Mermaid CLI MCP servers exist, all functioning as interfaces between AI assistants and the official Mermaid CLI (mmdc) to validate, render, and manage Mermaid diagrams [1][2][3][4]. These servers typically use Puppeteer for headless browser rendering and provide tools for syntax validation and exporting diagrams to formats like PNG, SVG, or PDF [1][3][5][6].

Installation generally requires Node.js and the global package `@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli` [1][2][3][5]. Configuration involves adding the server to your MCP client’s (e.g., Claude Desktop, Cursor) configuration file, often using `npx` or a local path to the server executable [2][3][4][6]. Common implementations include those by andrewginns [1], Narasimhaponnada [2], abekdwight [3], and hustcc [4].
